title: Bovorian

'''Bovorians''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sapient]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] indigenous to [[Bovo Yagen (planet)|Bovo Yagen]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Bovorians were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that was thought to have evolved from flying [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]]. For that reason, their most striking features were their [[Leather/Legends|leather]]-like, membranous vestigial wings. With the [[Limb/Legends|arm]] stretched out from the body, that part of their anatomy looked like the wing of a [[Mynock/Legends|mynock]], a [[Silicon/Legends|silicon]]-based parasite commonly found across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The Bovorians, however, could not fly, and their wings were only leftovers of their evolutionary process.<ref name="AE" />

Overall, Bovorians had a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] body characterized by a single head, a torso and four limbs attached to it. Their bodies were slightly thinner and longer than those of [[Human/Legends|Humans]], with an average height ranging from 1.8 to 2.3 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. The Bovorians were noted for their acute sense of hearing that gave them the ability to detect movement from up to a [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] away. They could also see in the [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] spectrum, thus allowing them to see in complete darkness as long as there were heat sources nearby.<ref name="AE" />

Like some other species that included the [[Vor]]s and the [[Chadra-Fan/Legends|Chadra-Fan]], the Bovorians were fifteen times more likely than others to catch pollution-induced respiratory ailments such as [[lungrot]], [[silicalung]], [[hemorrhagic molting]] and [[dioxo-brionchiectasis shock]].<ref name="HNN49" />

==History==
The Bovorians evolved on the jungle-covered planet of [[Bovo Yagen (planet)|Bovo Yagen]],<ref name="AE" /> in the [[Kailion sector/Legends|Kailion sector]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> They were believed to have evolved from non-sentient flying mammals, whence their distinctive membranous wings that had lost much of their ancestral function.<ref name="AE" /> Due to their sensitive hearing, heavy industry was banned on the home world. This led to the planet [[Trellar Yagen (planet)|Trellar Yagen]] being the hub of the mining and processing activity in their system.<ref name="CTD">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''</ref>

==Bovorians in the galaxy==
{{Quote|In an examination of Coruscant middle-class districts conducted by the Rhire Medical Academy, findings indicated that air pollutants caused by government industrial centers and civic traffic led to 15 times increased likelihood of debilitating respiratory ailments in the identified species.|HoloNet News Life report|<ref name="HNN49" />}}
By the time of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], there were Bovorians who lived on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. That year, the [[Rhinnal State Medical Academy]] released a report of middle-class districts on Coruscant, identifying ten species under the risk of being affected with diseases from the pollution. That list included the Bovorians, and the information was relayed in the [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49|forty-ninth issue]] of the 531st HoloNet News volume.<ref name="HNN49" />

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'', it was revealed that the Bovorians' homeworld of Bovo Yagen was only christened so after being discovered by an Imperial explorer of the same name.<ref name="CTD" /> However, in the [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' 49]], they were already called "Bovorians" at the time of the Separatist Crisis, three years before the rise of the Galactic Empire.<ref name="HNN49" />
